description: |-
  Creates and manages an AI Gateway: a curated stack of Anypoint policies applied to an existing API Manager instance
  	so that single Terraform resource expresses a complete LLM proxy. Internally the composite resolves the declared

---

# anypoint_ai_gateway (Resource)

Creates and manages an AI Gateway: a curated stack of Anypoint policies applied to an existing API Manager instance
		so that single Terraform resource expresses a complete LLM proxy. Internally the composite resolves the declared
		blocks to the right mix of inbound + outbound policies on the MuleSoft public mule4 policies group, in a
		known-good execution order.

		The underlying Exchange asset bound to `api_instance_id` must be of LLMAsset type — every LLM-class policy
		in the catalog (everything except `agent-connection-telemetry` and `rate-limiting`) declares
		`assetTypes: ['llm']` and Anypoint rejects the POST otherwise.

		For surgical control of any single policy use the underlying `anypoint_apim_policy_*` resources directly —
		the composite leaves them alone.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Minimal: bedrock-anthropic LLM gateway
resource "anypoint_ai_gateway" "minimal" {
  org_id          = var.org_id
  env_id          = var.env_id
  api_instance_id = anypoint_apim_mule4.llm_api.id

  llm_provider {
    type = "bedrock-anthropic"
    bedrock {
      aws_region            = "us-east-1"
      aws_access_key_id     = var.aws_access_key_id
      aws_secret_access_key = var.aws_secret_access_key
    }
  }
}

# Full-featured: prompt guard + model-based routing + telemetry + LLM-token rate limit
resource "anypoint_ai_gateway" "prod" {
  org_id          = var.org_id
  env_id          = var.env_id
  api_instance_id = anypoint_apim_mule4.llm_api.id

  llm_provider {
    type = "bedrock-anthropic"
    bedrock {
      aws_region            = "us-east-1"
      aws_access_key_id     = var.aws_access_key_id
      aws_secret_access_key = var.aws_secret_access_key
      timeout               = 60000
    }
  }

  prompt_guard {
    type = "semantic-openai"
    semantic_openai {
      openai_url             = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
      openai_api_key         = var.openai_api_key
      openai_embedding_model = "text-embedding-3-small"
      threshold              = 0.85
      deny_topics            = ["harm", "illegal", "violence"]
    }
  }

  routing {
    type = "model-based"
    model_based {
      supported_vendors = ["anthropic", "openai"]
    }
  }

  llm_rate_limit {
    maximum_tokens              = 50000
    time_period_in_milliseconds = 60000
    key_selector                = "#[attributes.headers['client-id']]"
  }

  telemetry {
    enabled         = true
    source_agent_id = "prod-gateway"
  }

  asset_versions = {
    llm_proxy_core                       = "1.0.1"
    bedrock_anthropic_transcoding_policy = "1.0.0"
    bedrock_llm_provider_policy          = "1.0.3"
    semantic_prompt_guard_policy_openai  = "1.0.1"
    model_based_routing                  = "1.0.1"
    llm_token_rate_limit                 = "1.0.1"
    agent_connection_telemetry           = "1.0.0"
  }
}

output "ai_gateway_applied_policies" {
  value = anypoint_ai_gateway.prod.applied_policies
}

output "ai_gateway_external_drift" {
  value = anypoint_ai_gateway.prod.external_policies_detected
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_instance_id` (String) The API Manager instance id to attach the gateway policies to. Reference `anypoint_apim_mule4.<x>.id` or `anypoint_apim_flexgateway.<x>.id`. The underlying Exchange asset must be of LLMAsset type for the LLM-class policies (every policy in the composite except `agent-connection-telemetry` and `rate-limiting`) to apply — Anypoint enforces this at policy POST time and surfaces a 400 if the asset is not LLM-typed.
- `env_id` (String) The environment id where the api instance is defined.
- `llm_provider` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) The upstream LLM provider configuration. Resolves to the provider + transcoding policy pair (or provider-only for `bedrock` pass-through). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--llm_provider))
- `org_id` (String) The organization id where the api instance is defined.

### Optional

- `asset_versions` (Map of String) Optional per-policy version override. Keys are snake_case-ified asset_ids (e.g. `bedrock_llm_provider_policy`). Falls through to the composite's known-good defaults when omitted.
- `last_updated` (String) The last time this resource has been updated locally.
- `llm_rate_limit` (Block List, Max: 1) Token-aware rate limit. Maps to `llm-token-rate-limit`. Mutually exclusive with `request_rate_limit`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--llm_rate_limit))
- `prompt_guard` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional input filtering block. Pick one guard sub-block matching `type`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--prompt_guard))
- `request_rate_limit` (Block List, Max: 1) Generic request-count rate limit. Maps to `rate-limiting`. Mutually exclusive with `llm_rate_limit`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--request_rate_limit))
- `routing` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional upstream-routing block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--routing))
- `telemetry` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional `agent-connection-telemetry` block. Always applied at inbound order 1 when present. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--telemetry))

### Read-Only

- `applied_policies` (List of Object) Computed list of policies the composite owns on the api_instance, with their resolved order and version. Order is per-injection-point. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--applied_policies))
- `external_policies_detected` (Boolean) True when the api_instance carries policies the composite does not recognise. Composite leaves them untouched on Update/Delete.
- `id` (String) Composite identifier: {org_id}/{env_id}/{api_instance_id}/ai_gateway.

<a id="nestedblock--llm_provider"></a>
### Nested Schema for `llm_provider`

Required:

- `bedrock` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Configuration for the `bedrock-llm-provider-policy`. Required when `type` is `bedrock` or `bedrock-anthropic`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--llm_provider--bedrock))
- `type` (String) Provider family. `bedrock` calls Bedrock directly (no transcoding); `bedrock-anthropic` applies the `bedrock-anthropic-transcoding-policy` so OpenAI-shaped client requests are translated to Anthropic Claude requests. OpenAI and Gemini providers will land in a later release.

<a id="nestedblock--llm_provider--bedrock"></a>
### Nested Schema for `llm_provider.bedrock`

Required:

- `aws_region` (String) AWS region (`awsRegion`).

Optional:

- `aws_access_key_id` (String, Sensitive) Static AWS Access Key ID (`awsAccessKeyId`). Provide either this or `aws_access_key_id_selector`.
- `aws_access_key_id_selector` (String) DataWeave selector resolving the AWS Access Key ID at runtime (`awsAccessKeyIdSelector`).
- `aws_secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) Static AWS Secret Access Key (`awsSecretAccessKey`).
- `aws_secret_access_key_selector` (String) DataWeave selector for the AWS Secret Access Key (`awsSecretAccessKeySelector`).
- `aws_session_token` (String, Sensitive) Optional AWS Session Token for temporary credentials (`awsSessionToken`).
- `service_name` (String) AWS service name for signing requests (`serviceName`). Defaults to `bedrock`.
- `timeout` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds for the Bedrock API call (`timeout`).



<a id="nestedblock--llm_rate_limit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `llm_rate_limit`

Required:

- `key_selector` (String) DataWeave expression identifying the rate-limit key (`keySelector`).
- `maximum_tokens` (Number) Maximum tokens per window (`maximumTokens`).
- `time_period_in_milliseconds` (Number) Window size in milliseconds (`timePeriodInMilliseconds`).


<a id="nestedblock--prompt_guard"></a>
### Nested Schema for `prompt_guard`

Required:

- `semantic_openai` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Configuration for the `semantic-prompt-guard-policy-openai`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--prompt_guard--semantic_openai))
- `type` (String) Guard family. `semantic-openai` applies `semantic-prompt-guard-policy-openai`. Other guard families (regex, semantic-huggingface, bedrock-guardrails) will land in a later release.

<a id="nestedblock--prompt_guard--semantic_openai"></a>
### Nested Schema for `prompt_guard.semantic_openai`

Required:

- `deny_topics` (List of String) List of denied topics (`denyTopics`).
- `openai_api_key` (String, Sensitive) OpenAI API key (`openaiApiKey`).
- `openai_embedding_model` (String) OpenAI embedding model identifier (`openaiEmbeddingModel`).
- `openai_url` (String) OpenAI embeddings endpoint URL (`openaiUrl`).
- `threshold` (Number) Cosine-similarity threshold (`threshold`) above which a prompt is blocked.

Optional:

- `openai_provider` (String) Optional OpenAI provider override (`openaiProvider`).
- `timeout` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds (`timeout`).



<a id="nestedblock--request_rate_limit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `request_rate_limit`

Required:

- `rate_limits` (Block List, Min: 1) List of rate-limit windows. Each entry maps to one element of the policy's `rateLimits` array. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--request_rate_limit--rate_limits))

Optional:

- `clusterizable` (Boolean) Whether the rate limit applies cluster-wide (`clusterizable`).
- `expose_headers` (Boolean) Whether to expose rate-limit headers in responses (`exposeHeaders`).
- `key_selector` (String) Optional DataWeave expression identifying the rate-limit key (`keySelector`). When unset, a global counter is used.

<a id="nestedblock--request_rate_limit--rate_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `request_rate_limit.rate_limits`

Required:

- `maximum_requests` (Number) Max requests per window (`maximumRequests`).
- `time_period_in_milliseconds` (Number) Window size in milliseconds (`timePeriodInMilliseconds`).



<a id="nestedblock--routing"></a>
### Nested Schema for `routing`

Required:

- `model_based` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Configuration for the `model-based-routing` policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--routing--model_based))
- `type` (String) Routing family. `model-based` applies `model-based-routing`. Semantic routing families will land in a later release.

<a id="nestedblock--routing--model_based"></a>
### Nested Schema for `routing.model_based`

Required:

- `supported_vendors` (List of String) List of supported provider vendors (`supportedVendors`). Routing decides upstream based on the inbound `model` field matching one of these.

Optional:

- `fallback` (String) Optional fallback target identifier (`fallback`) when no rule matches.



<a id="nestedblock--telemetry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `telemetry`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) When `false`, the telemetry policy is not applied.
- `source_agent_id` (String) Identifier emitted by the telemetry policy (`sourceAgentId`).


<a id="nestedatt--applied_policies"></a>
### Nested Schema for `applied_policies`

Read-Only:

- `asset_id` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `injection_point` (String)
- `order` (Number)
- `version` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# anypoint_ai_gateway is a composite over the api_instance's policies.
# Import is deferred to a follow-up release; for v1.11.0 manage the composite
# via terraform from the start.
#
# Composite id shape (for reference, once import lands):
#   {org_id}/{env_id}/{api_instance_id}/ai_gateway
#
# example (DOES NOT WORK in v1.11.0 — import handler not yet implemented):
#   terraform import anypoint_ai_gateway.prod aa1f55d6-.../7074fcdd-.../20935984/ai_gateway
```
